Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 23 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. AyaFlow: A high-performance, eBPF-based network traffic analyzer written in Rust 27 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1292 upvotes, 247 comments
  4. The Failure of the Thermodynamics of Computation (2010) 33 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. CSS is DOOMed 451 upvotes, 106 comments
  6. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 60 upvotes, 11 comments
  7. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 33 upvotes, 5 comments
  8. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 59 upvotes, 17 comments
  9. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 135 upvotes, 50 comments
  10. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 402 upvotes, 173 comments
  11. Netscape News Feed Straight Out of the Late 00s 11 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 154 upvotes, 63 comments
  13. A nearly perfect USB cable tester 209 upvotes, 102 comments
  14. Siclair Microvision (1977) 44 upvotes, 16 comments
  15. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 77 upvotes, 33 comments
  16. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 329 upvotes, 220 comments
  17. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 192 upvotes, 128 comments
  18. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 742 upvotes, 581 comments
  19. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. The bot situation on the internet is worse than you could imagine 114 upvotes, 76 comments
  21. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 203 upvotes, 157 comments
  22. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 17 upvotes, 7 comments
  23. Twice this week, I have come across embarassingly bad data 52 upvotes, 42 comments
  24. First Western Digital, now Sony: The tech giant suspends SD card sales 9 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Show HN: BreezePDF – Free, in-browser PDF editor 12 upvotes, 7 comments
  26. Show HN: Sheet Ninja – Google Sheets as a CRUD Back End for Vibe Coders 51 upvotes, 53 comments
  27. VR Is Not Dead 7 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. TSA lines are so out of control that travelers are hiring line-sitters 64 upvotes, 90 comments
  29. Scientific audio equipment analysis with analyzer shows no difference in quality 29 upvotes, 50 comments
  30. When Do We Become Adults, Really? 30 upvotes, 55 comments

Last generated 2:00am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
